title: Luzula mutabilis
intro: Luzula mutabilis, also known as the Variable Woodrush, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sedge family (Juncaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe, Asia, and North America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Luzula mutabilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal infusions, teas, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as cough syrups and expectorant preparations.
taxonomy: Luzula mutabilis is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Juncaceae. It is commonly classified as a genus of the order Poales. The plant is further classified under the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Liliopsida. Its classification is also known as an aquatic flowering plant due to its habitat.
